rationale: The repo already ships specs and code for infographic HTML output, multi-tab infographics, dataset-support-agenttalk, the agent-skill-system, and a skill-registry. The request is to compose these existing primitives into a single-step OutputMode (or alternative) rather than greenfield design. Research must map the current OutputMode enum, the Infographic templates, the Skill payload, the dataset-retention path, and the two-step infographic handler so the proposal can decide between OutputMode / Toolkit / template-name routing.

queries.intent: Discover the OutputMode enum and how it is consumed by AgentTalk / agent handler
Read the current Infographic output format to see how the LLM is steered today
Read the InfographicHTML output format — most likely the existing analogue of what the user wants
Inspect the outputs/formats package entry point to see how a format is registered
Read the infographic template registry — the user proposes 'name a template'
Read the infographic Pydantic model to understand the layout/data contract
Read the infographic helpers used by the current 2-step rendering flow
Read the infographic handler — this IS the current 2-step (follow-up) endpoint
Read the agent handler (likely the AgentTalk entrypoint) to see how output_mode is selected
Read the artifact handler — HTML infographic artifacts probably persist through it
Read the AgentTalk envelope test to learn the response contract
Read the output_generation prompt — this is where output_mode steers LLM behavior
Read the Skill model to see what a Skill currently carries (instructions, templates, datasets?)
Read the Skill mixin to learn how a Skill is invoked at bot runtime
Read the Skill loader to learn how skills are discovered / registered
Read the Skill tools layer — this is where the LLM-facing tool wrappers live
Read the prior brainstorm 'infographic-html-output' — earlier thinking on this exact axis
Read the prior brainstorm 'multi-tab-infographic' — same problem space, multi-section layout
Read the prior 'dataset-support-agenttalk' spec — datasets-in-response is the missing link
Read the existing infographic-html-output spec — what is already shipped today
Read the skill-registry spec — interplay with the Skill that drives the layout
Read the integration test for agent + dataset path — proves dataset retention shape
Read the infographic handler test to confirm the 2-step flow is still active
Grep for OutputMode call sites so we know everywhere the new mode must integrate
Recent git activity on the outputs package — what is in flight right now
Recent git activity on the skills package — current state of Skill primitives
Recent git activity on infographic handler — was the 2-step flow recently changed?

expected_findings: OutputMode is a Pydantic Enum / Literal with at least: text, json, markdown, infographic (or similar). Adding a new mode requires changes here + in handlers/agent.py + in outputs/formats registry.
outputs/formats/infographic_html.py already produces HTML — likely the building block, but it is invoked from the post-answer follow-up path, not from the primary answer.
The Skill model carries an instruction template + dataset declarations, but does NOT yet route into a one-shot infographic OutputMode.
handlers/infographic.py is a separate POST endpoint (the 2-step flow): existing infographic generation runs after the agent's text answer.
dataset-support-agenttalk has already made multiple datasets returnable in the AgentTalk envelope — but they are not yet wired into the infographic post-step.
The skill-registry and Skill primitives can already carry a 'template name' — Option C (skill names a template) is mostly a wiring change, not a new architecture.
Real open question: where the LLM gets instructed to bind generated datasets to the named template's slots (cards, charts) — this is the gap the proposal must close.
